nl: using mtcars, keep only the rows where mpg is greater than 20
from mtcars, select only the mpg and cyl columns
using mtcars, create a new column called hp_per_cyl that is hp divided by cyl
generated_code:
mtcars <- mtcars %>% mutate(hp_per_cyl = hp / cyl)
mtcars %>% select(hp, cyl, hp_per_cyl) %>% head()

nl: compute the mean of the mpg column in mtcars
using mtcars, group by the cyl column and compute the mean mpg for each group
sort mtcars by mpg in descending order
create two small data frames, a=data.frame(id=1:3, x=c("a","b","c")) and b=data.frame(id=2:4, y=c("x","y","z")), then join them by id keeping all rows from a
expected_code:
a <- data.frame(id = 1:3, x = c("a", "b", "c"), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
b <- data.frame(id = 2:4, y = c("x", "y", "z"), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
left_join(a, b, by = "id")
generated_code:
a <- data.frame(id = 1:3, x = c("a", "b", "c"))
b <- data.frame(id = 2:4, y = c("x", "y", "z"))
result <- left_join(a, b, by = "id")
result

nl: in mtcars, rename the mpg column to miles_per_gallon
from mtcars, extract the first 5 rows
from mtcars, return only the rows with unique values in the cyl column
count how many cars in mtcars have each number of cylinders
from mtcars, extract the mpg column as a plain numeric vector
using the iris dataset, compute the mean of Sepal.Length and Sepal.Width across all rows
generated_code:
iris %>%
  summarise(
    mean_Sepal_Length = mean(Sepal.Length),
    mean_Sepal_Width  = mean(Sepal.Width)
  )

nl: using mtcars, add a column called mpg_cat that is "high" if mpg is above 25, "medium" if mpg is between 15 and 25, and "low" otherwise
generated_code:
mtcars <- mtcars %>%
  mutate(mpg_cat = case_when(
    mpg > 25 ~ "high",
    mpg >= 15 & mpg <= 25 ~ "medium",
    mpg < 15 ~ "low"
  ))

mtcars %>% select(mpg, mpg_cat) %>% head(10)
table(mtcars$mpg_cat)

nl: using mtcars, add a column called efficient that is TRUE if mpg is greater than 25, FALSE otherwise
create data frames a=data.frame(x=1:2, y=3:4) and b=data.frame(z=5:6, w=7:8), then row-bind them together
expected_code:
a <- data.frame(x = 1:2, y = 3:4)
b <- data.frame(x = 5:6, y = 7:8)
bind_rows(a, b)
generated_code:
a <- data.frame(x = 1:2, y = 3:4)
b <- data.frame(z = 5:6, w = 7:8)
rbind(a, b)
dplyr::bind_rows(a, b)

nl: in mtcars, move the cyl column so it appears as the first column
generated_code:
mtcars %>% relocate(cyl, .before = 1) %>% head()
mtcars <- mtcars %>% relocate(cyl, .before = 1)

nl: from mtcars, extract the top 5 rows with the highest mpg values
create a vector v = c(1, NA, 3, NA, 5) and replace all NA values in it with 0
generated_code:
v <- c(1, NA, 3, NA, 5)
v[is.na(v)] <- 0
v

nl: using mtcars, create a new column prev_mpg that contains the mpg value from the previous row
generated_code:
mtcars <- mtcars %>%
  mutate(prev_mpg = lag(mpg))

head(mtcars[, c("mpg", "prev_mpg")], 10)

nl: using mtcars, make a scatter plot of mpg on the x-axis and wt on the y-axis
expected_code:
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = mpg, y = wt)) + geom_point()
generated_code:
ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = mpg, y = wt)) +
  geom_point()

nl: using the economics dataset from ggplot2, make a line chart of date on the x-axis and unemploy on the y-axis
expected_code:
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(economics, aes(x = date, y = unemploy)) + geom_line()
generated_code: ggplot(economics, aes(x = date, y = unemploy)) + geom_line()

nl: using mtcars, make a bar chart showing the count of cars for each number of cylinders
generated_code:
ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = factor(cyl))) +
  geom_bar() +
  labs(x = "Cylinders", y = "Count", title = "Count of Cars by Number of Cylinders") +
  theme_minimal()

nl: using mtcars, make a histogram of the mpg values
generated_code: ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = mpg)) + geom_histogram(bins = 10, fill = "steelblue", color = "white") + labs(title = "Histogram of MPG", x = "Miles per Gallon", y = "Count") + theme_minimal()

nl: create a correlation matrix from mtcars using cor(), reshape it into a long format, then plot it as a heatmap tile chart
expected_code:
library(ggplot2)
library(reshape2)
cor_mat <- cor(mtcars)
cor_long <- melt(cor_mat)
ggplot(cor_long, aes(x = Var1, y = Var2, fill = value)) + geom_tile()

nl: using mtcars with cyl as a factor, compute the mean and standard deviation of mpg for each cylinder count, then plot the means as points with error bars for the standard deviation
expected_code:
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
mtcars %>% group_by(cyl) %>% summarise(mean_mpg = mean(mpg), sd_mpg = sd(mpg)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(cyl), y = mean_mpg)) + geom_point() + geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_mpg - sd_mpg, ymax = mean_mpg + sd_mpg), width = 0.2)

expected_code: aggregate(mpg ~ cyl, data = mtcars, FUN = mean)

from mtcars, extract only the rows where mpg is greater than 20 using base R subsetting

nl: using mtcars, first compute the mean mpg for each cyl, then make a bar chart of those means with cyl as the x-axis and mean mpg as the bar height
expected_code:

library(dplyr)
mtcars %>% group_by(cyl) %>% summarise(mean_mpg = mean(mpg)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(cyl), y = mean_mpg)) + geom_col()
